extra.message: User input from params['id'][0] is directly concatenated into SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection attacks. An attacker can manipulate the 'id' parameter to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

User-supplied username and password are directly concatenated into SQL query string in the AuthBypass class. This allows SQL injection attacks to bypass authentication. An attacker can use payloads like ' OR '1'='1 to bypass login.

User input from params['domain'][0] is directly interpolated into a shell command string passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. This allows OS command injection. An attacker can use payloads like '; cat /etc/passwd to execute arbitrary commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The do_BDR method executes self.path[1:] directly as a shell command via subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. This allows arbitrary OS command execution via the HTTP request path.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User input from params.get('msg', '') is directly returned as HTML content without any sanitization or encoding. This allows reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript via the 'msg' parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Comments retrieved from the database are rendered directly into HTML table cells without sanitization. Combined with the INSERT at line 88 which stores user input, this enables stored XSS attacks where malicious scripts persist in the database and execute for all users viewing the guestbook.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied base64-encoded data is passed directly to pickle.loads() without validation. Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to arbitrary code execution. The RCE class in this file demonstrates how malicious pickle payloads can execute os.system commands.

User input from params.get('path') is passed directly to open() after only checking for '://' scheme. An attacker can use path traversal sequences like '../../../etc/passwd' to read arbitrary files on the filesystem.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled URL from params.get('path') is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() without validation. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks, enabling attackers to make requests to internal services or access cloud metadata endpoints.

User input from params.get('path', '/') is directly interpolated into JavaScript redirect without validation. This allows open redirect attacks where attackers can redirect users to malicious websites, useful for phishing campaigns.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User input from params.get('path', '/') is directly interpolated into JavaScript redirect in the SessionFixation class. This allows open redirect attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: Session ID can be set directly from URL parameter params.get('session'). This enables session fixation attacks where an attacker sets a known session ID on a victim's browser before they authenticate, then hijacks the session after login.

SSL/TLS security options are deliberately weakened: SSLv3 is re-enabled (disabled by default due to POODLE vulnerability), compression is enabled (CRIME attack vector), and cipher server preference is disabled. These settings create multiple known attack vectors.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
CWE-757: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation

extra.message: X-XSS-Protection header is set to '0', explicitly disabling browser XSS filtering. This removes a defense-in-depth protection against reflected XSS attacks.

Content-Security-Policy is set to "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'" which allows all resources from any source and permits inline JavaScript. This effectively neutralizes CSP's protections against XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials 'dsvpwa'/'dsvpwa' are used as a backdoor authentication mechanism. Hardcoded credentials cannot be changed without modifying code and are discoverable by attackers.

State-changing operation (email update) is performed via GET request without CSRF protection. An attacker can craft a malicious link that changes a victim's email address when visited, enabling Cross-Site Request Forgery.
Destructive action (account deletion) is performed via GET request without CSRF protection. An attacker can trick a logged-in user into deleting their account by visiting a malicious link.
